description: Forms of rule, governance structures, and political systems.
factions: Republic
Elected officials governing on behalf of the citizenry, though backroom deals often shape true policy.
City-State
A sovereign city commanding the land around it and fiercely guarding its independence from neighboring powers.
Council
A circle of representatives endlessly debating and bargaining, where every vote is a traded favor.
Parliament
Elected lawmakers clashing in open session, wielding rhetoric and alliances to bend the nation's course.
Theocracy
High priests ruling with divine mandate, where questioning them means questioning the gods themselves.
Oligarchy
A handful of power brokers dividing control among themselves and freezing out everyone beneath them.
Democracy
A system in which every citizen casts a vote, yet demagogues and mob passions test it daily.
Autocracy
One ruler holding absolute power, with every law, pardon, and execution flowing from their word alone.
Dictatorship
A tyrant crushing dissent through fear and tolerating no rival voice.
Monarchy
A crowned sovereign ruling by birthright, forever balancing noble ambitions against the weight of tradition.
Junta
Military officers who have seized the throne and now govern by decree, daring anyone to challenge their grip.
Regency
A regent steering the realm in an absent ruler's name, tempted daily to keep that power for good.
Aristocracy
Noble bloodlines monopolizing authority and passing titles from parent to child across generations.
Plutocracy
The wealthiest families buying policy and posts, turning gold into the only currency of power.
Meritocracy
A system where rank follows demonstrated skill, breeding ruthless competition among those clawing for advancement.
Technocracy
Scholars and engineers dictating policy through expertise while dismissing popular opinion as ignorant noise.
Hegemony
One dominant state bending its neighbors to its will without outright conquest -- demanding compliance through sheer influence.
Imperium
Supreme authority, often divine or arcane, radiating from a single seat of power that none dare defy.
Magocracy
Archmages ruling from spell-warded towers, where political rank rises only as high as one's magical talent.
Gerontocracy
The eldest elders commanding absolute respect and stifling change in favor of ancient precedent.
Stratocracy
Generals governing as heads of state, with military rank serving as the only path to political power.
Kleptocracy
Rulers openly looting the treasury and funneling public wealth into private vaults while the populace starves.
Timocracy
A system granting power only to landowners, leaving those without property neither voice nor standing.
Nomocracy
Written law reigning supreme, binding even rulers to codes no individual may overrule.
Polyarchy
Power splintered across many competing centers, each blocking the others in an uneasy deadlock.
Diarchy
Two co-rulers sharing the throne, their rivalry and cooperation shaping every decree.
Triarchy
Three rulers dividing power among themselves, each watching the other two for signs of betrayal.
Tetrarchy
Four rulers carving the realm into quarters and forging fragile truces that crack under ambition.
Pentarchy
Five rulers sharing dominion, forming and breaking alliances in an ever-shifting balance of power.
Suzerainty
A weaker state bowing to its overlord's foreign policy while clinging to a thin veneer of self-rule.
Vassalage
A lesser lord sworn in fealty to a greater one, trading loyalty and tribute for protection.
Atabegate
A regent-governor ruling a province on behalf of a distant throne with near-total local authority.
Mamlukate
Former slave-soldiers who have seized the reins of power, now ruling the very masters who once owned them.
Timar
Warriors holding land grants in exchange for military service, tying sword and soil inseparably together.
Zamindari
Landed administrators collecting taxes and keeping order, growing rich as middlemen between crown and peasant.
Jagir
Nobles governing territories granted by the throne and balancing service to the crown with personal ambition.
Bureaucracy
Clerks and officials running the state through ledgers and procedure, where paperwork wields more power than swords.
Constitution
A binding charter of principles constraining rulers and guaranteeing rights, revered almost like scripture.
Assembly
Citizens or delegates gathering to debate and decide, their voices rising in argument before every vote.
Congress
Delegates from many regions convening to hammer out law and trade favors across faction lines.
Senate
Senior statesmen deliberating in an upper chamber, wielding seniority and tradition as political weapons.
Court
A sphere where judges dispense law or courtiers orbit a sovereign -- a whispered word shifting fortunes overnight.
Electorate
The voting populace capable of elevating or destroying leaders, courted and manipulated in equal measure.
Duchy
A domain commanded by a duke or duchess, balancing loyalty to the crown against dynastic ambitions.
